scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: the Data Export API is a fixed, small, read-oriented surface (surveys, survey questions, responses, respondent attributes, GDPR personal-data) documented in full at developers.survicate.com/data-export/{survey,response,respondent,personal-data}. 3 read streams cover every list-shaped endpoint that has its own discovery path (surveys; survey_questions and responses fan out over every survey id). Respondent-scoped endpoints and the personal-data endpoints require a respondent_uuid/email obtained from an external source (there is no 'list all respondents' or 'list all emails' endpoint to fan out over), so they are excluded as requires_elevated_scope rather than modeled as streams. No write endpoints exist beyond the GDPR personal-data DELETE, which is excluded as destructive_admin.

excluded.reason: single-survey detail; every field this endpoint returns (id, type, name, created_at, enabled, responses, launch, plus author/folder/first_response_at/last_response_at) is a superset of the surveys list stream's per-item object already covered by that stream, keyed by the same id
single-response detail; identical record shape to the responses stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

no endpoint exists to enumerate respondent_uuid values across a workspace; a lone attributes-by-id GET with no discovery path is not a syncable stream without a respondent_uuid already in hand from an external source (e.g. one embedded in a responses-stream record)

same respondent_uuid discovery gap as the attributes endpoint above; also a duplicate record shape of the survey-scoped responses stream, which already yields every response for every discoverable survey
GDPR personal-data counters lookup keyed by a single email address supplied by the caller; not a list-shaped/discoverable resource and has no natural incremental or full-refresh catalog semantics
irreversibly deletes all survey responses, respondent records, and connected-service (Insights Hub) data for the given email address; a GDPR-erasure action, never a reverse-ETL-safe mutation
